schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Mode Decomposition in 3D Spherical Coordinates (The Math Backbone): δ(x): δ(x)

theory.canonical_statement: For a scalar field in 3D (density contrast \(\delta(\mathbf{x})\)), expand in spherical coordinates \((r,\hat{n})\) where \(\hat n\) is direction: \[ \delta(\mathbf{x})=\delta(r,\hat n) =\sum_{\ell=0}^{\infty}\sum_{m=-\ell}^{\ell}\int_0^\infty \! dk \; \delta_{\ell m}(k)\, j_\ell(kr)\, Y_\ell^m(\hat n). \tag{1} \] - \(j_\ell(kr)\) are spherical Bessel functions (radial eigenfunctions of \(\nabla^2\) in flat 3D). - \(Y_\ell^m(\hat n)\) are spherical harmonics (angular eigenfunctions on \(S^2\)). - \(\delta_{\ell m}(k)\) are expansion coefficients (complex).
theory.technical_interpretation: Mode Decomposition in 3D Spherical Coordinates (The Math Backbone): δ(x): δ(x) is a symbolic expression, written δ(x). It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from δ, x. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
